div1.entryFree: Devote, vb. (used only in the partic. --ed) to consecrate, to addict: he hath --d and given up himself to the contemplation . . ., Oth. II, 3, 321. --ed yours, Lucr. Dedic. Oth. II, 3, 321 a true --ed pilgrim, Gentl. II, 7, 9. the substance of your perfect self is else --ed, IV, 2, 125. LLL I, 1, 280. this is your --d friend, All's IV, 3, 264. thy poor --d suppliant, R3 I, 2, 207. == pious, holy: to stop --d charitable deeds, R3 I, 2, 35.
